query.original: Does a Barbarian lose their Rage bonus damage if they cast a spell while raging?
query.used: Does a Barbarian lose their Rage bonus damage if they cast a spell while raging?

retrieved_nodes.preview:
### Rage

In battle, you fight with primal ferocity. On your turn, you can enter a rage as a bonus action.

While raging, you gain the following benefits if you aren't wearing heavy armor:

- You have

### Relentless Rage

Starting at 11th level, your rage can keep you fighting despite grievous wounds. If you drop to 0 hit points while you're raging and don't die outright, you can make a DC 10 Const

#### Frenzy

Starting when you choose this path at 3rd level, you can go into a frenzy when you rage. If you do so, for the duration of your rage you can make a single melee weapon attack as a bonus a

#### Mindless Rage

Beginning at 6th level, you can't be charmed or frightened while raging. If you are charmed or frightened when you enter your rage, the effect is suspended for the duration of the

### Persistent Rage

Beginning at 15th level, your rage is so fierce that it ends early only if you fall unconscious or if you choose to end it.

answer: Yes, a Barbarian loses their Rage bonus damage if they cast a spell while raging. The context states that "If you are able to cast spells, you can't cast them or concentrate on them while raging."

correctness.feedback: The generated answer is relevant to the user query but contains a significant mistake. It incorrectly states that a Barbarian loses their Rage bonus damage if they cast a spell, when in fact, a Barbarian cannot cast spells at all while raging. The reference answer correctly explains that spellcasting is not possible during rage, which the generated answer fails to convey accurately.
